request.prompt: a screen to delete my account

children.text: Are you sure? This is a permanent action.
Deleting your account will permanently remove all associated data, including projects, files, and user history. Please ensure you have backed up any necessary information before proceeding.

findings.rationale: Nested interactive elements create ambiguous click targets and are an accessibility violation: screen readers cannot determine intent and click handling varies across browsers.

findings.message: 'button' must carry non-empty direct text (its own `text` field — text in descendants does not count).
findings.rationale: The trigger button must present its label as its own text. Assistive technology reads the control's accessible name from the element itself, and protocol projections lift a trigger label from the label-bearing component's direct text — a label nested in a child (e.g. a badge) or absent entirely produces a control with no accessible name and an instance downstream emitters must refuse.

children.text: This will permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone.

findings.message: 'button' must carry non-empty direct text (its own `text` field — text in descendants does not count).
findings.rationale: The trigger button must present its label as its own text. Assistive technology reads the control's accessible name from the element itself, and protocol projections lift a trigger label from the label-bearing component's direct text — a label nested in a child (e.g. a badge) or absent entirely produces a control with no accessible name and an instance downstream emitters must refuse.

repairMessages:
Your surface violates 2 governance rule finding(s) of the "shadcn/ui" design system:

Violation 1: [rule.button-no-interactive-descendants / must]
  At: $.root.children[0].children[0].children[0].children[0] (component: button)
  Forbidden descendant 'button' inside 'button' (at $.root.children[0].children[0].children[0]).
  Why: Nested interactive elements create ambiguous click targets and are an accessibility violation: screen readers cannot determine intent and click handling varies across browsers.
Violation 2: [rule.trigger-carries-label / must]
  At: $.root.children[0].children[0].children[0] (component: button)
  'button' must carry non-empty direct text (its own `text` field — text in descendants does not count).
  Why: The trigger button must present its label as its own text. Assistive technology reads the control's accessible name from the element itself, and protocol projections lift a trigger label from the label-bearing component's direct text — a label nested in a child (e.g. a badge) or absent entirely produces a control with no accessible name and an instance downstream emitters must refuse.
